creator: fk@fabiankeil.de
text:
On a recent FreeBSD stable/11 the rescue binary content varies depending
on the number of cpu cores.

With a 2 core system I can reproduce the binaries from the official
11.4-BETA2 release, with a 4 core system I get different results:

SHA256 (/images/FreeBSD/FreeBSD-11.4-BETA2-amd64-r361067-official/rescue/cp) = 3c96ab697fc721c226f99971863bcadbbbc73ccfec1abe2f04cbce60e3276a89
SHA256 (/images/FreeBSD/FreeBSD-11.4-BETA2-amd64-r361067-rebuild-2cores-t520/rescue/cp) = 3c96ab697fc721c226f99971863bcadbbbc73ccfec1abe2f04cbce60e3276a89
SHA256 (/images/FreeBSD/FreeBSD-11.4-BETA2-amd64-r361067-rebuild-4cores/rescue/cp) = 06e0b2964901fa02ed2f4e02c1334cb82ce1d97f4a6fc7a2632a6f74b35955d1

Diffoscope seems to indicate that some offsets are different.
The output starts with:

--- FreeBSD/FreeBSD-11.4-BETA2-amd64-r361067-official/rescue/cp
+++ FreeBSD/FreeBSD-11.4-BETA2-amd64-r361067-rebuild-4cores/rescue/cp
â”śâ”€â”€ readelf --wide --sections {}
â”‚ @@ -1,16 +1,16 @@
â”‚  There are 19 section headers, starting at offset 0xa18bb0:
â”‚  
â”‚  Section Headers:
â”‚    [Nr] Name              Type            Addr             Off    Size   ES Flg Lk Inf Al
â”‚    [ 0]                   NULL            0000000000000000 000000 000000 00      0   0  0
â”‚    [ 1] .note.tag         NOTE            0000000000400190 000190 000030 00   A  0   0  4
â”‚    [ 2] .init             PROGBITS        00000000004001c0 0001c0 000018 00  AX  0   0  4
â”‚ -  [ 3] .text             PROGBITS        0000000000400200 000200 6dab08 00  AX  0   0 64
â”‚ -  [ 4] .fini             PROGBITS        0000000000adad08 6dad08 00000e 00  AX  0   0  4
â”‚ +  [ 3] .text             PROGBITS        0000000000400200 000200 6daa88 00  AX  0   0 64
â”‚ +  [ 4] .fini             PROGBITS        0000000000adac88 6dac88 00000e 00  AX  0   0  4
â”‚    [ 5] .rodata           PROGBITS        0000000000adb000 6db000 216bd8 00   A  0   0 4096
â”‚    [ 6] .eh_frame_hdr     PROGBITS        0000000000cf1bd8 8f1bd8 0245ec 00   A  0   0  4
â”‚    [ 7] .eh_frame         X86_64_UNWIND   0000000000d161c8 9161c8 0bb90c 00   A  0   0  8
â”‚    [ 8] .tdata            PROGBITS        0000000000fd2000 9d2000 000068 00 WAT  0   0 16
â”‚    [ 9] .tbss             NOBITS          0000000000fd2070 9d2068 000840 00 WAT  0   0 16
â”‚    [10] .ctors            PROGBITS        0000000000fd2070 9d2070 0000c0 00  WA  0   0  8
â”‚    [11] .dtors            PROGBITS        0000000000fd2130 9d2130 000018 00  WA  0   0  8
â”śâ”€â”€ readelf --wide --debug-dump=frames {}
â”‚ @@ -9894,21179 +9894,21195 @@
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚  
â”‚ -00005ee0 0000002c 00005ee4 FDE cie=00000000 pc=0043c780..0043d57c
â”‚ +00005ee0 0000002c 00005ee4 FDE cie=00000000 pc=0043c780..0043d4c3
â”‚    DW_CFA_advance_loc: 1 to 0043c781
â”‚    DW_CFA_def_cfa_offset: 16
â”‚    DW_CFA_offset: r6 (rbp) at cfa-16
â”‚    DW_CFA_advance_loc: 3 to 0043c784
â”‚    DW_CFA_def_cfa_register: r6 (rbp)
â”‚    DW_CFA_advance_loc: 16 to 0043c794
â”‚    DW_CFA_offset: r3 (rbx) at cfa-56
â”‚    DW_CFA_offset: r12 (r12) at cfa-48
â”‚    DW_CFA_offset: r13 (r13) at cfa-40
â”‚    DW_CFA_offset: r14 (r14) at cfa-32
â”‚    DW_CFA_offset: r15 (r15) at cfa-24
â”‚ -  DW_CFA_advance_loc2: 3554 to 0043d576
â”‚ +  DW_CFA_advance_loc2: 3369 to 0043d4bd
â”‚    DW_CFA_def_cfa: r7 (rsp) ofs 8
â”‚ -  DW_CFA_advance_loc: 1 to 0043d577
â”‚ +  DW_CFA_advance_loc: 1 to 0043d4be
â”‚    DW_CFA_def_cfa: r6 (rbp) ofs 16
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop
â”‚    DW_CFA_nop

The whole diff in text format has a size of 217M so I'm not attaching it.

I first saw the issue after rebasing ElectroBSD on r360524 and confirmed
that it still exists in r360986.

The problem does not exist in r357496 and earlier but I don't yet know
the exact revision when the problem was introduced.

I can't reproduce the issue on FreeBSD-13.0-CURRENT amd64
using the 20200514-r361019 snapshot.

I haven't tested 12/stable.
